text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH FDG (WHOLE BODY) - POST-TREATMENT RESPONSE ASSESSMENT

EXAMINATION: FDG-PET/CT, whole body from vertex to mid-thigh

INDICATION: 52-year-old female with HPV-positive (p16+) oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue, T2N1M0 (AJCC 8th edition, Stage I, HPV-positive classification). Treated with definitive concurrent chemoradiation: intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT) with simultaneous integrated boost to a total dose of 70 Gy in 35 fractions, delivered over 7 weeks, concurrent with Platinol 100 mg/m2 (2 full cycles plus 1 dose-reduced cycle at 75 mg/m2). This study is performed approximately 10 weeks following the final radiation fraction for post-treatment metabolic response assessment. Comparison is made to the pre-treatment staging PET-CT.

TECHNIQUE: Intravenous injection of 11.4 mCi (421 MBq) of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG). Images were acquired 62 minutes following radiotracer injection. Blood glucose at time of injection: 91 mg/dL (within acceptable range). Whole-body PET emission data was acquired in 3D mode from vertex to mid-thigh and fused with simultaneously acquired low-dose CT data used for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. IV iodinated contrast was administered for the diagnostic CT component. Oral contrast was administered. The study was reviewed on dedicated PET and CT workstations with multiplanar reconstructions and co-registered fusion images. Maximum standardized uptake values (SUVmax) are reported using lean body mass correction.

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK - PRIMARY SITE:
The left base of tongue, which on the prior staging examination demonstrated an intensely hypermetabolic soft tissue mass with SUVmax of 14.2, is now markedly changed in appearance. There is no discrete soft tissue mass or nodular thickening at the left base of tongue. The treatment field demonstrates expected post-radiation mucosal edema and inflammatory changes involving the oropharynx and pharyngeal walls bilaterally, with diffuse, symmetrical, low-level mucosal FDG uptake that is physiologic in distribution and does not exceed background soft tissue activity. No focal area of intense or asymmetric FDG avidity is identified at the primary tumor site. No residual hypermetabolic lesion is appreciable at the vallecula or adjacent supraglottic structures. The mucosa appears thickened and edematous in the oropharynx bilaterally, a finding consistent with acute and subacute radiation mucositis. The findings are consistent with complete metabolic resolution at the primary site.

HEAD AND NECK - REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
The previously described left level IIA cervical lymph node, which had measured 2.6 cm on prior imaging and demonstrated intense FDG hypermetabolism (SUVmax 11.8) with a central photopenic area corresponding to central necrosis, is now markedly involuted. A small residual soft tissue density measuring approximately 1.1 cm in short axis is present in the left level IIA nodal station. This nodal remnant does not demonstrate meaningful FDG uptake above background activity, with a measured SUVmax of 1.8, which is below the threshold of mediastinal blood pool reference activity (SUVmax of the descending aorta measured at 2.1 on this examination). The morphology and metabolic quiescence of this nodal remnant is consistent with residual fibrotic or treated nodal tissue without viable tumor. No other cervical lymph nodes in the left neck demonstrate pathologic FDG uptake or suspicious morphology. The right cervical lymph node chains (levels I-V), bilateral retropharyngeal nodes, bilateral parapharyngeal spaces, and bilateral submandibular and parotid gland regions show no hypermetabolic foci. Radiation-related changes in the bilateral parotid glands are present, manifesting as reduced size and diffuse low-level background uptake, consistent with post-radiation atrophy.

SKULL BASE AND INTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES:
No hypermetabolic skull base lesion. No intracranial mass or abnormal FDG uptake beyond physiologic cortical activity. The visualized calvarium is intact.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland appears slightly reduced in volume and demonstrates diffusely low-level, heterogeneous uptake, a pattern that can be seen in the setting of prior radiation exposure. No focal nodular hypermetabolism. Clinical correlation for radiation-induced thyroid dysfunction is recommended, noting the patient's pre-existing hypothyroidism on levothyroxine.

ORO- AND NASOPHARYNX / LARYNX:
No focal hypermetabolic lesion in the nasopharynx, hypopharynx, or larynx to suggest synchronous second primary or new metachronous lesion. Mild symmetrical FDG activity within the posterior pharyngeal walls is present, consistent with post-radiation inflammatory change. The glottis and subglottis appear normal metabolically.

CHEST:
Lungs: No pulmonary nodules or masses with abnormal FDG uptake are identified. No solid or part-solid pulmonary nodules suspicious for metastatic disease. Mild bibasilar atelectatic changes present. No pleural effusion.
Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal and bilateral hilar lymph nodes are normal in size and demonstrate no pathologic hypermetabolism. Physiologic cardiac and vascular activity noted. No anterior mediastinal mass.
Bones: No hypermetabolic osseous lesion in the thoracic cage, thoracic spine, or visualized clavicles to suggest metastatic involvement.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Liver: Normal size and homogeneous parenchymal FDG distribution. No focal lesion.
Spleen: Normal size and uniform uptake. No suspicious focus.
Pancreas: No focal hypermetabolic lesion identified.
Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal without pathologic uptake.
Kidneys/Ureters/Bladder: Physiologic tracer excretion through the urinary tract. No suspicious renal parenchymal or perirenal lesion.
Gastrointestinal tract: Physiologic heterogeneous bowel activity. No focal lesion suspicious for malignancy.
Retroperitoneal and pelvic lymph nodes: No enlarged or hypermetabolic retroperitoneal or pelvic lymph nodes identified.
Osseous structures, abdomen and pelvis: No hypermetabolic skeletal focus suspicious for metastatic disease in the lumbar spine, pelvis, sacrum, or proximal femora.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous or deep soft tissue masses with abnormal uptake. Mild diffuse cutaneous FDG uptake in the left lateral neck region consistent with post-radiation dermatitis in the treatment field.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. COMPLETE METABOLIC RESPONSE AT THE PRIMARY SITE: The previously hypermetabolic left base of tongue mass is no longer identifiable. No FDG-avid tissue is present at the primary tumor site. The oropharynx demonstrates diffuse, symmetric, low-level post-radiation inflammatory changes without any focal or asymmetric area of increased uptake to suggest residual viable malignancy.

2. COMPLETE METABOLIC RESPONSE AT THE LEFT LEVEL IIA LYMPH NODE: The previously enlarged, necrotic, and intensely hypermetabolic left level IIA lymph node has markedly regressed. A small fibrotic-appearing nodal remnant remains, measuring approximately 1.1 cm, with FDG activity below mediastinal blood pool reference levels (SUVmax 1.8). This is consistent with a treated, metabolically inactive nodal remnant without evidence of residual disease.

3. NO NEW SITES OF DISEASE: No new hypermetabolic foci in the neck, chest, abdomen, or pelvis to suggest distant metastatic disease or a new synchronous malignancy.

4. POST-TREATMENT CHANGES: Findings consistent with post-radiation inflammatory changes throughout the oropharynx and bilateral parotid glands. Mild post-radiation dermatitis in the left neck soft tissues.

OVERALL IMPRESSION: Complete metabolic response following definitive concurrent chemoradiation for HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma. No imaging evidence of residual locoregional or distant disease.

Attending Radiologist: [Signature on file]
Nuclear Medicine Physician: [Co-signed on file]
